revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:走水神社 (神戸市)]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| name = Hashiudo Shrine
| location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|qid=Q686802|lt=Chūō-ku, Kobe|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}} Motomachi-dōri 5-6-1
| Latitude = 34 | Latitude minutes = 41 | Latitude seconds = 5.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N 
| Longitude = 135 | Longitude minutes = 10 | Longitude seconds = 53.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E
| Map Country Code = JP
| deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qid=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}
| consecration_year = Heian Period
| type = Village Shrine (村社)
| festival = January 18–19 (Yakuyoke-sai), July 24–25 (Tenjin-sai)
| Main Hall = 
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image = File:Hashiudo Jinja2.JPG
| Map = Kobe#Japan Hyōgo
| location = {{ill|Chūō-ku|qid=Q686802|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|34.684861|N|135.181444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}

{{Right|
[[File:Hashiudo Jinja1.JPG|thumb|none|200px|The reading "Hashiudo" is notable for its difficulty.]]
[[File:Cattle at shito shrine.jpg|thumb|none|200px|Hashiudo Shrine’s guardian ox statues, dedicated to Tenjin.]]
}}

'''Hashiudo Shrine''' (走水神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kobe|en|Chūō-ku, Kobe|qid=Q686802|lt=Chūō-ku, Kobe|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is situated one block south of the Motomachi shopping street along an east-west road.

The shrine is the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the former Hashiudo Village and has a history spanning over 1,100 years. Known for prayers for abundant harvests, health, business prosperity, and learning, it is also celebrated as a shrine for calligraphy. A {{ill|fudezuka|ja|筆塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fudezuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (brush monument) stands beside the main shrine. Since {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} is one of the enshrined deities, guardian ox statues, rather than traditional lion-dog statues, are present. The shrine’s notable festivals are the {{ill|Yakuyoke-sai|ja|厄除祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuyoke-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in January and the {{ill|Tenjin Matsuri|en|Tenjin Matsuri|qid=Q5371810|lt=Tenjin Festival|1=Tenjin Matsuri}} in July. Its previous status under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine.

== History ==
Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the area now known as Motomachi-dōri 5-chōme included a {{ill|Buddhist temple|ja|仏教寺院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺院|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺院|1=Buddhist temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5393308}} called Gokuraku-ji and a Hachiman Shrine, leading to the area being named "Hachiman Town." In {{ill|1874|en|1874|qid=Q7761|lt=1874|1=1874}}, it was renamed "Motomachi-dōri," and in {{ill|1875|en|1875|qid=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}}, the Hachiman Shrine and a nearby Tenman Shrine were merged. Since the location corresponded to the former Hashiudo Village, the newly merged shrine was named Hashiudo Shrine.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}

== Etymology of "Hashiudo" ==
The name "Hashiudo" has two potential origins:
1. The former Ujigawa River (宇治川), known for forming "rushing whirlpools" (走り渦) during rainfall, gave its name to the surrounding village, which became known as Hashiudo Village due to the damage caused by these whirlpools.
2. Another theory connects the name to the Hashihito clan (間人氏), who served the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Imperial Court of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. The clan’s settlement in the area led to the name "Hashihito," which later evolved into "Hashiudo," with the kanji 走水 applied to the name.

Notably, the Hashihito clan contributed to educational advancements in the area. In {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}}, Hashihito Taneko (間人たね子) founded the Hashiudo Childcare Center, continuing the clan’s legacy of education.

== Nearby Shrines ==
Different sections of Motomachi have varying {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}}:
* Motomachi-dōri 5–6-chōme: Hashiudo Shrine
* Motomachi-dōri 4-chōme: {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (Kobe)|ja|厳島神社 (神戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine (Kobe)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Motomachi-dōri 3-chōme: {{ill|Sannomiya Shrine (Kobe)|ja|三宮神社 (神戸市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomiya Shrine (Kobe)|qq=}}
<gallery>
File:Itsukusima Jinja(Kobe).JPG|Itsukushima Shrine
File:Sannomiya Jinja(kobe).JPG|Sannomiya Shrine
</gallery>

== Nearby Landmarks and Cultural Facilities ==
* {{ill|Hyōgo Prefectural Milestone|ja|兵庫縣里程元標|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefectural Milestone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Motomachi Shopping Street|ja|元町商店街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomachi Shopping Street|1=Motomachi Shopping Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387984}}
* {{ill|Koiwa-suji|ja|鯉川筋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koiwa-suji|1=Koikawa-suji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11674236}}
* {{ill|Hongan-ji Kobe Betsuin|ja|本願寺神戸別院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hongan-ji Kobe Betsuin|1=Hongan-ji Kobe Betsu-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11521062}} (Modern Temple)
* {{ill|Nankin-machi|ja|南京町 (神戸)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankin-machi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南京町 (神戸市)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南京町 (神戸市)|1=Nankin-machi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q912392}}
* {{ill|Kyū Kyoryūchi|ja|旧居留地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyū Kyoryūchi|1=Kyukoryuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11086968}}
** {{ill|Kobe City Museum|en|Kobe City Museum|qid=Q3330666|lt=Kobe City Museum|1=Kobe City Museum}}
** {{ill|Kobe Lamp Museum|ja|神戸らんぷミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Lamp Museum|1=Kobe Lamp Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589727}}
** {{ill|Kyū Kyoryūchi Fifteen Bankan|ja|旧居留地十五番館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyū Kyoryūchi Fifteen Bankan|1=Former Foreign Settlement Building No. 15|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511038}}
* {{ill|Meriken Park|en|Meriken Park|qid=Q5365128|lt=Meriken Park|1=Meriken Park}}
** {{ill|Kobe Maritime Museum|ja|神戸海洋博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobe Maritime Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Kobe Maritime Museum|qid=Q5364119}}
** {{ill|Fish Dance|ja|フィッシュダンス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fish Dance|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フィッシュ・ダンス|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フィッシュ・ダンス|1=Fish Dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11332422}}


== See Also ==
